outcome_contract.why: Products can go live on the XA store without photos, breaking the shopping experience. P1 priority ‚Äî store not ready for real customers until resolved.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: The xa-b storefront only displays products that are publishable (live or out_of_stock with complete images); draft products with missing images never appear in any listing.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Draft products removed from all listing pages
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Draft products removed from all listing pages
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Draft products removed from all listing pages
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: xa-b listing pages clean; no broken-image cards possible

engineering_coverage.applicable: Add-to-cart path for draft products eliminated at both layers
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Add-to-cart path unchanged in xa-b (drafts still in model)
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Add-to-cart path for draft products eliminated in xa-b
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: `getAvailableStock` never sees a draft product ‚Äî no cart flow risk

engineering_coverage.applicable: Tests needed for publish route (mixed-state snapshot) + xa-b normalizeProductStatus
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Tests needed for publish route only
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Both test gaps must be closed; unit tests in xa-uploader publish route test suite + xa-b catalog model tests

engineering_coverage.applicable: Published catalog contract will no longer include `status:"draft"` entries; xa-b model never contains drafts
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Published catalog contract will no longer include drafts
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Publish contract still includes drafts; xa-b silently drops them
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Contract behavior change for publish route; xa-b silently drops drafts as safety net

engineering_coverage.applicable: Both fixes are simple filter additions; no hot-path impact
Safe: git revert on either fix is clean; no data migration
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Per-fix rollback possible; no migration risk

stage_payload.decision_summary: The per-product publish API route (`POST /api/catalog/publish`) passes all snapshot products ‚Äî including `"draft"`-state products with no images ‚Äî to `buildCatalogArtifactsFromDrafts`. This results in draft products appearing in the published catalog contract. The xa-b storefront makes this worse by accepting `"draft"` as a valid `normalizeProductStatus` return value and not filtering by status in `filterAndSortProducts`. The decision is: how to close this leak with minimum blast radius and maximum defense-in-depth.
goals: Ensure the per-product publish route only includes publishable products in the catalog contract
Ensure xa-b storefront never displays draft products in any listing, regardless of contract content
Eliminate any path where a product with no images can appear on the live storefront
non_goals: Changing the UI gate (`showMakeLiveAction`) ‚Äî already correct
Changing the sync route ‚Äî already filters correctly; reference implementation only
Adding server-side `isPublishReady` check to the publish API beyond the existing filter
Changes to caryina ‚Äî already safe via `includeDraft: false` in `listShopSkus`
Investigating product detail page (PDP) separately ‚Äî xa-b fix covers this by exclusion (no draft product can be navigated to if none enter the catalog model)
Changing the published contract format for live/out_of_stock products
constraints: Must not change the published contract format for `"live"` or `"out_of_stock"` products
Must not break the sync route behavior
Fix must be confined to xa-uploader publish route + xa-b catalog model; no caryina changes
Import of `isCatalogPublishableState` and `deriveCatalogPublishState` required in publish route (already exported from `packages/lib/src/xa/catalogWorkflow.ts`)
assumptions: After the xa-b fix, `normalizeProductStatus("draft")` returns `null` ‚Äî products never enter the catalog model

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Draft products must not reach the storefront via any path
Server side is root cause; client side is defense layer against future contract drift
Fix must not alter live/out_of_stock contract outputs
Regression test gap must be closed for both surfaces
Small team ‚Äî minimal code surface change preferred

options_considered.Description: Server-side filter in publish route + xa-b `normalizeProductStatus` returns null for "draft"
options_considered.Upside: Complete fix at both layers; defense-in-depth; mirrors sync route exactly; xa-b cannot display drafts even if contract ever regresses
options_considered.Downside: Two touch points (publish route + xa-b); slightly more code
options_considered.Key risks: Negligible ‚Äî both changes are additive filters, no existing behavior for live/out_of_stock is altered

options_considered.Description: Server-side filter in publish route only (single fix)
options_considered.Downside: xa-b would display drafts if contract ever includes them again (e.g., via a future publish route variation or direct API call); no defense-in-depth
options_considered.Key risks: If another publish path is added later, the leak could reopen silently

options_considered.Description: xa-b `normalizeProductStatus` returns null for "draft" only (client-side only)
options_considered.Upside: xa-b is protected regardless of contract content
options_considered.Downside: Root cause unresolved ‚Äî publish contract still writes draft products; operator-facing APIs still return draft products to any other consumer
options_considered.Key risks: Publish contract correctness violated; other potential consumers of the contract also see drafts

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A ‚Äî fix both the publish route (server-side root cause) and xa-b's `normalizeProductStatus` (client-side defense layer).
chosen_approach.Why this wins: The publish route fix is the root cause fix ‚Äî it mirrors the already-correct sync route behavior (`isCatalogPublishableState(deriveCatalogPublishState(product))`). The xa-b fix adds defense-in-depth: if any publish path ever regresses or a draft appears in the contract from any source, xa-b can never display it. The combined fix is the only option that closes the gap completely. Both changes are additive; no existing behavior for live/out_of_stock products is altered.
chosen_approach.**Option C (client-side only)** ‚Äî Not: any other contract consumer (future tools, analytics, admin views) would see draft products. Root cause must be addressed server-side.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (server-side only)** ‚Äî Viable but leaves xa-b with no defense layer. If a future publish path or direct API call ever produces draft entries in the contract, xa-b would display them silently. Defense-in-depth is worth one additional null return in `normalizeProductStatus`.

**Option C (client-side only)** ‚Äî Not viable as a sole fix. The publish contract correctness violation is real: any other contract consumer (future tools, analytics, admin views) would see draft products. Root cause must be addressed server-side.

sequencing_constraints: TASK-01 and TASK-02 are independent ‚Äî can execute in parallel or either order
TASK-03 depends on TASK-01; TASK-04 depends on TASK-02
risks_to_carry_forward: Import path for `isCatalogPublishableState`/`deriveCatalogPublishState` must be verified in publish route context

areas.label: Per-product publish
areas.current: `executeCloudPublish` passes ALL snapshot products to `buildCatalogArtifactsFromDrafts`; draft-state products (no images) enter the live catalog contract
areas.future: 1. Operator clicks Make Live (UI gate: isPublishReady required). 2. `executeCloudPublish` reads full snapshot. 3. `updateProductPublishStateInCloudSnapshot` sets target product publishState=live in snapshot. 4. Snapshot products are pre-filtered: `isCatalogPublishableState(deriveCatalogPublishState(product))` ‚Äî only live/out_of_stock pass. 5. Filtered products only passed to `buildCatalogArtifactsFromDrafts`. 6. Contract written with publishable products only.
areas.steady: Sync route already correct; caryina already correct; published contract format for live/out_of_stock products unchanged
areas.seams: Empty-catalog edge case: if all products are draft at publish time, contract is written with zero products ‚Äî acceptable; no guard needed

areas.label: xa-b storefront catalog parsing
areas.current: `normalizeProductStatus("draft")` returns `"draft"`; draft products enter catalog model and appear in all listing pages
areas.future: 1. Fetch catalog JSON. 2. `parseXaCatalogModel` normalizes products. 3. `normalizeProductStatus("draft")` now returns `null`. 4. Products with status=null are filtered out at parse time. 5. Draft products never enter `XaCatalogSnapshot.products`. 6. `filterAndSortProducts` and `getAvailableStock` only ever see live/out_of_stock products.
areas.steady: normalizeProductStatus behavior for live/out_of_stock unchanged; all filtering logic unchanged
areas.seams: If contract still contains drafts (e.g., before server-side fix is deployed), xa-b fix silently drops them ‚Äî correct behavior
